reasoning: Q1 is 16x slower than target (771ms vs 48ms) with strong foundations but critical CPU-side inefficiencies. With 1 iteration left, aggressively pursuing ALL high-impact optimizations: aligned SIMD loads (10-20%), HDD prefetching (15-30%), bit-shift hash (5-8%), SIMD horizontal aggregation (15-25%), and reduced morsel size (5-12%). Combined ~45-75% speedup targets ~175-425ms range. The code is correct; optimization is purely performance-focused.
focus_areas: Replace unaligned SIMD loads with aligned loads (_mm512_load_si512) - verify 64-byte alignment or add explicit prefetching
Add MADV_WILLNEED hints in MMapColumn::open() to coordinate HDD readahead and eliminate seek storms
Replace multiplication in perfect hash with bit-shift operations: (returnflag << 1) | linestatus
Implement SIMD horizontal aggregation with _mm512_mask_add to stay in vector domain longer
Reduce morsel size from 50K to 25-30K rows to fit L2 cache (1.28MB) for better temporal locality
strategy_notes: Final iteration - maximize impact across multiple CPU-side bottlenecks simultaneously. Priorities 1-3 and 7 are low-risk foundational optimizations. Priority 4 (SIMD horizontal aggregation) is medium-risk but high-reward - the Query Optimizer should carefully implement vector accumulation with proper reduction at morsel boundaries. Focus on eliminating scalar processing overhead in the hot path (60M row evaluations). The data is in warm page cache (1.7GB fits in 376GB RAM), so bottleneck is memory access patterns and computation efficiency, not raw I/O bandwidth. Hardware: 64 cores, AVX-512, 44MB L3 cache, 32MB L2 total. Target aggressive 45-75% combined speedup to reach competitive range with DuckDB.
notes: Skip priority 5 (two-phase filtering) - minimal benefit at 98.8% selectivity. Skip priority 6 (zone maps) - only 1-2% gain. Skip priority 9 (Kahan summation removal) - high risk of validation failures. The selected combination focuses on proven, measurable CPU optimizations that compound multiplicatively.
